info.name: SAP_GYS
info.description:
# SAP GYS

Postman collection for **SAP GYS** integration endpoints.

## Environment setup

- **Base URL**: `https://{{dev}}`
    
- **Auth token variable**: `{{token}}`
    
    - Run **Token** first to generate/refresh the token and store it in the active environment.
        

## Authentication

Unless otherwise stated, requests require **Bearer Token** authentication:

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

## Folder overview

- **GET Schema**: Master-data / reference lookups (read-only)
    
- **POST Schema**: SRM-style actions and submissions (create/change/release/cancel/update)
    

## Notes

- This collection does **not** change URLs or methods; it documents existing requests only.
    
- Query parameters shown in URLs are examples/defaults and can be adjusted per use case.

exec:
### Purpose
Fetch Material Master data with example filters prefilled in the URL.

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

### Example query params (from URL)
- `MatCode` (example: `''`)
- `MatGroup` (example: `''`)
- `MatType` (example: `'ZGCR'`)
- `MRPContrl` (example: `''`)
- `Plant` (example: `''`)

Fetch payment status for **Payment Proposal Create & Run** flow.

```http

```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

### Example query params (from URL)

exec:
# GET Schema

Read-only **master/reference** endpoints.

## Typical usage
- Use these requests to fetch master data (e.g., material groups, plants, GL accounts) used to validate or populate fields for SRM POST operations.

## Authentication
Most endpoints are expected to use:

```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

## Base URL
All requests use:

- `https://{{dev}}`
Obtain an authentication token from the GYS backend.
- **No Bearer token required** (this is the auth endpoint).
- Typically `Content-Type: application/json` (depends on the request body configured).
- Uses a **POST** body to submit credentials (fields are defined in the request body).
- Expected behavior: stores the access token into the active environment variable `token` (used as `{{token}}` by other requests).

response.name: Success
Fail

### Purpose
Create a vendor in SRM via the GYS integration.

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
- `Content-Type: application/json` (expected for POST payloads)

### Body notes
- Request body contains the vendor data to create (see **Body** tab for exact fields).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

- POST body should include the PR identifier and the fields to change (see **Body** tab for configured fields).

request.description:
## Purpose

Create a Purchase Order (PO) in SRM.

## Endpoint

`POST https://{{dev}}/srm/create-po`

- `{{dev}}`: Base host for the target environment (see **GYS Dev Environment**).
    

## Authentication

Bearer token.

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

- `{{token}}`: Access token value.
    

## Headers

- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
    
- `Content-Type: application/json`
    

## Request body (JSON)

Top-level fields:

- `PoNumber` _(string)_: **Auto-generated by the backend**. Do **not** fill this field; providing a value may cause an error.
    
- `Zzspkno` _(string)_: Custom/reference field (business-specific).
    
- `Zzctrno` _(string)_: Contract/reference number (business-specific).
    
- `Zzpurty` _(string)_: Purchase type/category (business-specific).
    

Nested objects/arrays:

- `postPOHeaderSet` _(object)_: PO header data (document/vendor/payment terms/org/currency/incoterms/downpayment/document dates, etc.).
    
- `postPOItemSet` _(array of objects)_: PO item lines (material, quantity, unit, net price, plant, storage location, tax code, tolerances, indicators, etc.).
    
- `postPOScheduleSet` _(array of objects)_: Delivery schedule per PO item.
    
- `postPOPartnerSet` _(array of objects)_: Partner data.
    
- `postPOAdditionalSet` _(array of objects)_: Additional/custom item-level data.
    

### Field notes

- Date formats in this payload can vary by field (e.g., ISO `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` for some date-time fields, and `DD.MM.YYYY` for `DeliveryDate`). Follow the API’s expected format per field.
    
- Some fields are business-specific/custom (often prefixed with `Z\*` or `Zz\*`). Confirm allowed values with your SRM/SAP configuration.
    

## Example request body

> Inline comments removed (JSON does not support comments). Values below are illustrative. 
  

``` json
{
  "PoNumber": "001",
  "Zzspkno": "-",
  "Zzctrno": "002/SCRAP AGREEMENT/1/202",
  "Zzpurty": "RM",
  "postPOHeaderSet": {
    "DocType": "IMAT",
    "Vendor": "2301000113",
    "Pmnttrms": "Z007",
    "PurchOrg": "PROC",
    "PurGroup": "SM1",
    "Currency": "IDR",
    "ExchRate": "1",
    "Incoterms1": "FRA",
    "OurRef": "",
    "DownpayType": "M",
    "DownpayAmount": "12900000",
    "DownpayPercent": "20",
    "DownpayDuedate": "2026-03-26T00:00:00",
    "DocDate": "2026-03-11T00:00:00"
  },
  "postPOItemSet": [
    {
      "PoItem": "00010",
      "Material": "RIDR11000000000036",
      "ShortText": "",
      "Quantity": "100",
      "PoUnit": "KG",
      "NetPrice": "6450",
      "MatlGroup": "DI",
      "Plant": "1SM0",
      "StgeLoc": "S1R0",
      "Trackingno": "",
      "TaxCode": "2C",
      "PrntPrice": true,
      "OverDlvTol": "5",
      "GrInd": true,
      "IrInd": true,
      "ConfCtrl": "0001",
      "Incoterms1": "FRA",
      "ItemCat": " ",
      "Acctasscat": "",
      "PreqNo": "1100008061",
      "PreqItem": "00010",
      "PreqName": "Shiyan",
      "PckgNo": "0000000001"
    }
  ],
  "postPOScheduleSet": [
    {
      "PoItem": "00010",
      "SchedLine": "0001",
      "DeliveryDate": "30.04.2026",
      "Quantity": "100",
      "PreqNo": "1100008061",
      "PreqItem": "00010"
    }
  ],
  "postPOPartnerSet": [
    {
      "Partnerdesc": "Z2",
      "Langu": "EN",
      "Buspartno": "8888000284"
    }
  ],
  "postPOAdditionalSet": [
    {
      "Ebelp": "00010",
      "Zzeta": "2026-03-12T00:00:00"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

## Example success response (placeholder)

> Response shape may vary by backend implementation. 
  

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "PO created",
  "poNumber": "4500001234"
}

 ```

## Common error codes

- `400 Bad Request` — Validation error / invalid payload (e.g., providing a value for an auto-generated field such as `PoNumber`, invalid date formats, missing required fields).
    
- `401 Unauthorized` — Missing/invalid/expired `{{token}}`.
    
- `403 Forbidden` — Token is valid but not authorized for this action.
    
- `404 Not Found` — Wrong host/base URL (`{{dev}}`) or endpoint path.
    
- `409 Conflict` — Duplicate/conflicting data (implementation-specific).
    
- `500 Internal Server Error` — Unexpected backend error.

response.name: Success

exec:
### Purpose
Change/update an existing Purchase Requisition (PR) via SRM.

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
- `Content-Type: application/json`

### Body notes
- POST body should include the PR identifier and the fields to change (see **Body** tab for configured fields).

request.description:
## Purpose

Create a Purchase Order (PO) in SRM.

## Endpoint

`POST https://{{dev}}/srm/create-po`

- `{{dev}}`: Base host for the target environment (see **GYS Dev Environment**).
    

## Authentication

Bearer token.

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

- `{{token}}`: Access token value.
    

## Headers

- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
    
- `Content-Type: application/json`
    

## Request body (JSON)

Top-level fields:

- `PoNumber` _(string)_: **Auto-generated by the backend**. Do **not** fill this field; providing a value may cause an error.
    
- `Zzspkno` _(string)_: Custom/reference field (business-specific).
    
- `Zzctrno` _(string)_: Contract/reference number (business-specific).
    
- `Zzpurty` _(string)_: Purchase type/category (business-specific).
    

Nested objects/arrays:

- `postPOHeaderSet` _(object)_: PO header data (document/vendor/payment terms/org/currency/incoterms/downpayment/document dates, etc.).
    
- `postPOItemSet` _(array of objects)_: PO item lines (material, quantity, unit, net price, plant, storage location, tax code, tolerances, indicators, etc.).
    
- `postPOScheduleSet` _(array of objects)_: Delivery schedule per PO item.
    
- `postPOPartnerSet` _(array of objects)_: Partner data.
    
- `postPOAdditionalSet` _(array of objects)_: Additional/custom item-level data.
    

### Field notes

- Date formats in this payload can vary by field (e.g., ISO `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` for some date-time fields, and `DD.MM.YYYY` for `DeliveryDate`). Follow the API’s expected format per field.
    
- Some fields are business-specific/custom (often prefixed with `Z\*` or `Zz\*`). Confirm allowed values with your SRM/SAP configuration.

## Example success response (placeholder)

> Response shape may vary by backend implementation. 
  

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "PO created",
  "poNumber": "4500001234"
}

 ```

## Common error codes

- `400 Bad Request` — Validation error / invalid payload (e.g., providing a value for an auto-generated field such as `PoNumber`, invalid date formats, missing required fields).
    
- `401 Unauthorized` — Missing/invalid/expired `{{token}}`.
    
- `403 Forbidden` — Token is valid but not authorized for this action.
    
- `404 Not Found` — Wrong host/base URL (`{{dev}}`) or endpoint path.
    
- `409 Conflict` — Duplicate/conflicting data (implementation-specific).
    
- `500 Internal Server Error` — Unexpected backend error.

### Purpose
Release a Purchase Order (PO) in SRM.

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
- `Content-Type: application/json`

### Body notes
- POST body contains the release instruction/details (see **Body** tab).

exec:
### Purpose
Submit a Down Payment (DP) request via SRM.

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
- `Content-Type: application/json`

### Body notes
- POST body contains the DP request payload (see **Body** tab).

### Auth
```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

### Key headers
- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
- `Content-Type: application/json`

### Body notes
- POST body should identify which DP request to cancel (see **Body** tab).

item.name: Cancel Post Invoice (PO and Non PO)

exec: Change/update an existing Purchase Requisition (PR) via SRM.
- POST body should include the PR identifier and the fields to change (see **Body** tab for configured fields).

request.description:
## Purpose

Create a Purchase Order (PO) in SRM.

## Endpoint

`POST https://{{dev}}/srm/create-po`

- `{{dev}}`: Base host for the target environment (see **GYS Dev Environment**).
    

## Authentication

Bearer token.

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

- `{{token}}`: Access token value.
    

## Headers

- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
    
- `Content-Type: application/json`
    

## Request body (JSON)

Top-level fields:

- `PoNumber` _(string)_: **Auto-generated by the backend**. Do **not** fill this field; providing a value may cause an error.
    
- `Zzspkno` _(string)_: Custom/reference field (business-specific).
    
- `Zzctrno` _(string)_: Contract/reference number (business-specific).
    
- `Zzpurty` _(string)_: Purchase type/category (business-specific).
    

Nested objects/arrays:

exec:
# POST Schema

Action endpoints that **create/change/release/cancel/update** documents.

## Typical usage order
1. Run **Token** to obtain `{{token}}`.
2. Execute action requests as needed (PR create/change, vendor create, DP request/cancel, PO release, GR quantity update).

## Authentication
Unless the endpoint is explicitly an auth endpoint, use:

```http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

## Base URL
All requests use:

- `https://{{dev}}`

request.description:
## Purpose

Create a Purchase Order (PO) in SRM.

## Endpoint

`POST https://{{dev}}/srm/create-po`

- `{{dev}}`: Base host for the target environment (see **GYS Dev Environment**).
    

## Authentication

Bearer token.

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

- `{{token}}`: Access token value.
    

## Headers

- `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`
    
- `Content-Type: application/json`
    

## Request body (JSON)

Top-level fields:

- `PoNumber` _(string)_: **Auto-generated by the backend**. Do **not** fill this field; providing a value may cause an error.
    
- `Zzspkno` _(string)_: Custom/reference field (business-specific).
    
- `Zzctrno` _(string)_: Contract/reference number (business-specific).
    
- `Zzpurty` _(string)_: Purchase type/category (business-specific).
    

Nested objects/arrays:

- `postPOHeaderSet` _(object)_: PO header data (document/vendor/payment terms/org/currency/incoterms/downpayment/document dates, etc.).
    
- `postPOItemSet` _(array of objects)_: PO item lines (material, quantity, unit, net price, plant, storage location, tax code, tolerances, indicators, etc.).
    
- `postPOScheduleSet` _(array of objects)_: Delivery schedule per PO item.
    
- `postPOPartnerSet` _(array of objects)_: Partner data.
    
- `postPOAdditionalSet` _(array of objects)_: Additional/custom item-level data.
    

### Field notes

- Date formats in this payload can vary by field (e.g., ISO `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` for some date-time fields, and `DD.MM.YYYY` for `DeliveryDate`). Follow the API’s expected format per field.
    
- Some fields are business-specific/custom (often prefixed with `Z\*` or `Zz\*`). Confirm allowed values with your SRM/SAP configuration.
    

## Example request body

> Inline comments removed (JSON does not support comments). Values below are illustrative. 
  

``` json
{
  "PoNumber": "001",
  "Zzspkno": "-",
  "Zzctrno": "002/SCRAP AGREEMENT/1/202",
  "Zzpurty": "RM",
  "postPOHeaderSet": {
    "DocType": "IMAT",
    "Vendor": "2301000113",
    "Pmnttrms": "Z007",
    "PurchOrg": "PROC",
    "PurGroup": "SM1",
    "Currency": "IDR",
    "ExchRate": "1",
    "Incoterms1": "FRA",
    "OurRef": "",
    "DownpayType": "M",
    "DownpayAmount": "12900000",
    "DownpayPercent": "20",
    "DownpayDuedate": "2026-03-26T00:00:00",
    "DocDate": "2026-03-11T00:00:00"
  },
  "postPOItemSet": [
    {
      "PoItem": "00010",
      "Material": "RIDR11000000000036",
      "ShortText": "",
      "Quantity": "100",
      "PoUnit": "KG",
      "NetPrice": "6450",
      "MatlGroup": "DI",
      "Plant": "1SM0",
      "StgeLoc": "S1R0",
      "Trackingno": "",
      "TaxCode": "2C",
      "PrntPrice": true,
      "OverDlvTol": "5",
      "GrInd": true,
      "IrInd": true,
      "ConfCtrl": "0001",
      "Incoterms1": "FRA",
      "ItemCat": " ",
      "Acctasscat": "",
      "PreqNo": "1100008061",
      "PreqItem": "00010",
      "PreqName": "Shiyan",
      "PckgNo": "0000000001"
    }
  ],
  "postPOScheduleSet": [
    {
      "PoItem": "00010",
      "SchedLine": "0001",
      "DeliveryDate": "30.04.2026",
      "Quantity": "100",
      "PreqNo": "1100008061",
      "PreqItem": "00010"
    }
  ],
  "postPOPartnerSet": [
    {
      "Partnerdesc": "Z2",
      "Langu": "EN",
      "Buspartno": "8888000284"
    }
  ],
  "postPOAdditionalSet": [
    {
      "Ebelp": "00010",
      "Zzeta": "2026-03-12T00:00:00"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

## Example success response (placeholder)

> Response shape may vary by backend implementation. 
  

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "PO created",
  "poNumber": "4500001234"
}

 ```

## Common error codes

- `400 Bad Request` — Validation error / invalid payload (e.g., providing a value for an auto-generated field such as `PoNumber`, invalid date formats, missing required fields).
    
- `401 Unauthorized` — Missing/invalid/expired `{{token}}`.
    
- `403 Forbidden` — Token is valid but not authorized for this action.
    
- `404 Not Found` — Wrong host/base URL (`{{dev}}`) or endpoint path.
    
- `409 Conflict` — Duplicate/conflicting data (implementation-specific).
    
- `500 Internal Server Error` — Unexpected backend error.

response.name: Success - Invoice PO
Success - Invoice Non PO
